title: Solver
description: A pure function that produces output for a Sample. Solvers are cacheable by `(canonical_input, solver_id, version, model_ref)` because they hold no per-call state. Stateful tool-using counterparts are modeled as `Agent` (see agent.json) — the Solver/Agent split is deliberate (research/12 §1).

version.description: SemVer for the Solver's logic. Cache invalidation hinges on this.

kind.description: Solver flavor. `model` = a single ChatProvider call; `function` = pure code (no model); `composed` = a chain/pipeline of other Solvers.

prompt_template_ref.description: Optional reference into the PromptStore (content-addressed or named). Used by `model` Solvers.

params.description: Solver-specific configuration (temperature, system_message, retrieval flags, etc.). Open by design.
